findings.title: Base deployment can broadcast Base venue targets on the wrong chain
findings.suggested_fix: Read BASE_CHAIN_ID and compare it to block.chainid before vm.startBroadcast. Revert on mismatch, and add a deployment-script test that proves the Base-specific router deployment/configuration path cannot proceed on a non-Base chain.

findings.title: Native ETH leftovers make otherwise valid routes fail for non-payable contract senders
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit refund recipient, allow native dust to be wrapped to WETH, or reject routes that can leave route-local native ETH unless the caller can receive it. Cover a contract sender with no payable receive path and a partial native-wrap or native-leftover route.

residual_risks: I did not run forge or bun tests because this review was constrained to read-only commands except writing this artifact.
Universal Router V3/V4 behavior still depends on live Permit2 and Universal Router semantics; static review and mocks do not fully prove every production command path.

Secret scan matches in the reviewed diff were variable names, comments, or Doppler-wrapped command examples; I found no raw RPC URL, private key, mnemonic, explorer key, or secret embedded in the new public config, router artifacts, or DeployFameRouter broadcasts.
testing_gaps: Add a non-Base chain-id negative test for DeployFameRouter before broadcast starts.
Add a contract-sender native ETH refund test for partial NativeWrap/native-leftover routes.
Run the pinned Base fork route suite with Doppler-provided BASE_RPC before launch.
